name: Chandogya Upanishad
summary: One of the longest Upanishads, the Chandogya teaches through stories and dialogues. It contains the famous Tat Tvam Asi (That thou art) teaching of Uddalaka to his son Shvetaketu, and the story of Satyakama Jabala.

verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: तत् — जगत्कारणं ब्रह्म। त्वम् — प्रत्यगात्मा। असि — अभिन्नः। जीवब्रह्मैक्यं बोधयति।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: 'Tat' is Brahman, the cause of the universe. 'Tvam' is the individual Self. 'Asi' declares their identity. This mahavakya teaches the non-difference of the individual soul and Brahman.

verses.transliteration: om ity etad akṣaram udgītham upāsīta | om iti hy udgāyati | tasyopavyākhyānam ||1.1.1||
verses.english_translation: One should meditate on the syllable Om as the Udgitha. For one sings the Udgitha beginning with Om.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: उद्गीथः — सामगानस्य प्रधानभागः। ओंकारः तस्य वाचकः। तम् उपासीत।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: The Udgitha is the principal part of Sama chanting. Om is its indicator. One should meditate on Om as the Udgitha, knowing it as Brahman.

verses.sanskrit: स ह श्वेतकेतुरारुणेयः पञ्चविंशतिवर्षः पित्रा उक्तः — ब्रह्मचर्यं वस सोम्य ||६.१.१||
verses.transliteration: sa ha śvetaketur āruṇeyaḥ pañcaviṃśativarṣaḥ pitrā uktaḥ — brahmacaryaṃ vasa somya ||6.1.1||

word_meanings.meaning: live the life of a student

verses.english_translation: Shvetaketu, the grandson of Aruna, at twenty-five years of age, was told by his father: 'Live the life of a student, O dear one.'
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: श्वेतकेतुः — उद्दालकस्य पुत्रः — पित्रा ब्रह्मचर्याश्रमे प्रेषितः।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: Shvetaketu, son of Uddalaka Aruni, was sent by his father to study under a teacher. This begins the famous dialogue in which Uddalaka teaches his son the identity of Atman and Brahman through nine illustrations.
